description: Radiated emissions spike at clock harmonics because a fast net crosses a gap or split in its reference plane.
body: Failure mode: a high-speed trace routes over a slot, plane split, or void, forcing its return current into a long detour that enlarges the radiating loop. Detection signals: emissions failing at specific clock harmonics, a plane review showing a gap beneath the net, and improvement when the net is rerouted over solid copper. Do not add shielding to mask emissions while the return path is broken. Safer fallback: reroute the net over a continuous plane or bridge the split with stitching capacitors and re-scan emissions.
